creator: bcran@FreeBSD.org
text:
Attempting to move files from an NTFS filesystem to UFS causes a panic.

panic: System call rename returning with 1 locks held
cpuid = 0
KDB: enter: panic
[thread pid 1394 tid 100082]
Stopped at kdb_enter+0x30: leave

Stack trace:
kdb_enter
panic
syscall
Xint0x80_syscall() at Xint0x80_syscall+0x20
--- syscall (45, FreeBSD ELF32, ktrace), eip = 0x280d5dc7

Trying to mount root from ufs:/dev/ad0s1a
WARNING: / was not properly dismounted
sis0: Applying short cable fix (reg=f0)
sis0: Applying short cable fix (reg=e8)
sis0: Applying short cable fix (reg=e8)
sis0: Applying short cable fix (reg=e8)

Fix: 

Since it's not possible to remove files from an NTFS volume due to restrictions on writing to NTFS, don't try to move files from the filesystem.
How-To-Repeat: kldload ntfs
mount -t ntfs /dev/ad2s1 /mnt
cd /mnt
mv -iv * ~/data
